evals.prompt: Add a new fire spell with different range, damage, and mana cost, but otherwise reuse the existing fire pattern.
evals.expected_output: Keeps the ask on the ring-spell path, classifies it as pattern reuse, and names the correct content/runtime surfaces without inventing generic-ability or platform work.
expectations: Treats the ask as a ring spell
Classifies it as pattern reuse or equivalent low-scope spell work
Names ring-spell content and runtime definition surfaces

evals.prompt: Add a new spell that needs a projectile trail and impact burst nobody has authored yet.
evals.expected_output: Routes the spell to animation ownership work in addition to normal ring-spell authoring.
expectations: Treats the ask as a ring spell
Routes to adding-animation or names animation ownership surfaces explicitly
Does not stop at only the spell content file

evals.prompt: I want a combo spell that requires two schools before it can be studied. What else has to change?
evals.expected_output: Treats the ask as combo-school platform work, names presenter or study-surface limitations, and routes to mechanic-style expansion honestly.
expectations: Treats the ask as ring-spell work
Classifies it as combo-school or platform expansion
Names presenter, game-view, or ring-spell availability surfaces

evals.prompt: Add a generic sword ability that is not tied to rings.
evals.expected_output: Redirects away from the ring-spell path and points to the generic ability workflow.
expectations: Does not keep the answer on the ring-spell path
Avoids naming ring-spell-only surfaces as if they were required
